Complete reference of TypeScript interfa: Complete reference of TypeScript interfaces used in MBC CQRS Serverless framework.
This document provides a comprehensive r: This document provides a comprehensive reference of all TypeScript interfaces used in the MBC CQRS Serverless framework.
The primary interface for creating new c: The primary interface for creating new commands. Used when publishing new entities.
Used for partial updates. Only pk, sk, a: Used for partial updates. Only pk, sk, and version are required.
Options passed to command publishing met: Options passed to command publishing methods.
Represents the primary key for DynamoDB: Represents the primary key for DynamoDB items.
Invocation context containing Lambda/Exp: Invocation context containing Lambda/Express event and context.
HTTP request event structure from API Ga: HTTP request event structure from API Gateway or Express.
JWT token claims structure from Cognito.
Cognito groups the user belongs to: Cognito groups the user belongs to
Custom claim for roles JSON array: Custom claim for roles JSON array
The custom:roles claim contains a JSON a: The custom:roles claim contains a JSON array of role assignments
Empty tenant (\"\") means the role appli: Empty tenant ("") means the role applies globally
Specific tenant means the role applies o: Specific tenant means the role applies only to that tenant
User context class extracted from authen: User context class extracted from authentication token.
Extract user context from IInvoke or Exe: Extract user context from IInvoke or ExecutionContext
Unique request ID for tracing and idempo: Unique request ID for tracing and idempotency
Timestamp when the command was created: Timestamp when the command was created
Timestamp when the command was last upda: Timestamp when the command was last updated
User ID who created the command: User ID who created the command
User ID who last updated the command: User ID who last updated the command
IP address of the last updater: IP address of the last updater
Step Functions task token for async work: Step Functions task token for async workflows
The `taskToken` field is used when integ: The `taskToken` field is used when integrating with AWS Step Functions callback patterns. When a Step Functions state machine invokes your application with a task token, store it using [`CommandService.updateTaskToken()`](/docs/command-service#updatetasktoken). Later, use the token with AWS SDK's `SendTaskSuccessCommand` or `SendTaskFailureCommand` to signal task completion.
Base interface for data entities (read m: Base interface for data entities (read model).
Command partition key - references sourc: Command partition key - references source command record
Command sort key with version - referenc: Command sort key with version - references exact command version
Class implementing CommandModel for API: Class implementing CommandModel for API responses. Includes Swagger decorators.
Returns { pk, sk } for DynamoDB operatio: Returns { pk, sk } for DynamoDB operations
Class implementing DataModel for API res: Class implementing DataModel for API responses. Includes Swagger decorators.
Paginated list response for data queries: Paginated list response for data queries.
More items available - use result.lastSk: More items available - use result.lastSk for next page
Interface for implementing data synchron: Interface for implementing data synchronization handlers. Handles both forward (up) and rollback (down) operations.
Event structure from AWS Step Functions.
Input data passed to the state: Input data passed to the state
Partial type that requires specific keys: Partial type that requires specific keys
Custom claim for tenant group membership: Custom claim for tenant group memberships JSON array
Direct roles for the active tenant (excl: Direct roles for the active tenant (excludes group-derived roles)
Group IDs from custom:groups for the act: Group IDs from custom:groups for the active tenant
'SYNC' when the command was applied via: 'SYNC' when the command was applied via synchronous publish
`IGroupRoleResolver` was added in [versi: `IGroupRoleResolver` was added in [version 1.3.1](/docs/changelog#v131) for group-based role authorization.
The `custom:groups` claim was added to `: The `custom:groups` claim was added to `JwtClaims` in [version 1.3.1](/docs/changelog#v131) as part of group-based role authorization.
`tenantRoles` and `tenantGroupIds` were: `tenantRoles` and `tenantGroupIds` were added to `UserContext` in [version 1.3.1](/docs/changelog#v131) as part of group-based role authorization. `tenantRole` (singular) is kept for backward compatibility.
Interface for resolving group-to-role ma: Interface for resolving group-to-role mappings. Implement exactly one resolver per application and register it with `@GroupRoleResolver()`.
Active tenant for which roles are being: Active tenant for which roles are being resolved
Group IDs from the `custom:groups` JWT c: Group IDs from the `custom:groups` JWT claim for this tenant
Full JWT claims for additional context: Full JWT claims for additional context
Do not add @Injectable() — @GroupRoleRes: Do not add @Injectable() — @GroupRoleResolver() registers a singleton provider
Map group IDs to roles — fetch from DB,: Map group IDs to roles — fetch from DB, config, etc.
Sort key (e.g., \"ORDER#ORD001\") — no v: Sort key (e.g., "ORDER#ORD001") — no version suffix needed
No @version suffix — version is the sepa: No @version suffix — version is the separate field
Required: Current version for optimistic: Required: Current version for optimistic locking
Cognito user ID (from JWT sub claim): Cognito user ID (from JWT sub claim)
Sender address (uses default if not spec: Sender address (uses default if not specified)
Reserved for future use (not yet impleme: Reserved for future use (not yet implemented)
Disable the default DynamoDB data sync h: Disable the default DynamoDB data sync handler
Enable or disable default sequence contr: Enable or disable default sequence controller
